entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Tropical maize used to investigate adaptation differences between highland and lowland environments

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: The process of adjusting to different environmental conditions, specifically highland and lowland

entities.name: HIGHLAND
entities.description: High altitude environment characterized by lower temperatures

entities.name: LOWLAND
entities.description: Low altitude environment characterized by higher temperatures

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKERS
entities.description: Loci used to investigate genetic adaptation differences

entities.name: F2 POPULATION
entities.description: Population derived from the cross of two inbred lines used for phenotyping

entities.name: INBRED LINES
entities.description: Lines independently derived from highland and lowland maize germplasm

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic resources from highland and lowland maize

entities.name: FIELD TRIALS
entities.description: Replicated trials conducted at thermally diverse tropical testing sites

entities.name: TROPICAL TESTING SITES
entities.description: Thermally diverse sites ranging from lowland to extreme highland

entities.name: MEAN GROWING SEASON TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Temperature range from 13.2 to 24.6 degrees Celsius across testing sites

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: A trait closely related with adaptation analyzed in the study

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: A trait closely related with adaptation analyzed in the study

entities.name: YIELD COMPONENTS
entities.description: Components contributing to grain yield analyzed for adaptation

entities.name: FLOWERING TIME
entities.description: Days from sowing to male and female flowering

entities.name: TOTAL LEAF NUMBER
entities.description: A trait analyzed for adaptation

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: A trait analyzed for adaptation

entities.name: NUMBER OF PRIMARY TASSEL BRANCHES
entities.description: TBN, a trait analyzed for adaptation

entities.name: LINE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: Interaction observed for most traits across different sites

entities.name: GENOMIC COMPOSITION
entities.description: The proportion of marker alleles with the same origin

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: QTLs detected for various traits across sites

entities.name: DISEASE SCORES
entities.description: A trait measured at one site

entities.name: PLEIOTROPY
entities.description: The phenomenon where one gene influences multiple traits, suggested as the cause for QTL clustering

entities.name: THERMAL ADAPTATION
entities.description: Adaptation to specific thermal regimes

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A tool to reduce linkage drag and improve breeding for thermal adaptation

entities.name: LINKAGE DRAG
entities.description: The unintentional transfer of undesirable traits during breeding

relationships.description: MAIZE exhibits ADAPTATION to different environments
ADAPTATION occurs in HIGHLAND conditions
ADAPTATION occurs in LOWLAND conditions
MOLECULAR MARKERS used to investigate ADAPTATION
INBRED LINES derived from GERMPLASM

FIELD TRIALS used to measure YIELD COMPONENTS

Genetic basis of LINE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION reflected by QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI control BIOMASS
